//includeTags: We generate ONLY the routes a screen actually runs through. That is not scope discipline -- it is the
only thing that makes src/app/api/** trustworthy.
THE RULE: a route joins this list when, and only when, its C# gains a .Produces<T>(). Annotate first,
then generate. NEVER widen this list to 'fix' a missing method.
Why: an unannotated route returns `IResult` via `Results.Ok(x)`, and ApiExplorer CANNOT infer a schema
from that -- OpenAPI describes it as '200: OK' with no content. It also carries the default assembly tag
'TimesheetApp.Api', which is not in this list, so it is simply omitted. Add its tag WITHOUT annotating it
and you get a method typed `void` for an endpoint that in fact returns data: a client that LIES,
committed to the repo, that a later wave would reasonably trust.
AND: a route that IS annotated but ADMIN-GATED is a different lie -- correctly typed, and it 403s for
every ordinary user. Check .RequireAuthorization(AuthSetup.AdminPolicy) before you add a tag.
/api/users/all and /api/pca-contacts/all are admin-only and must NEVER appear here. A contract test
(OpenApiContractTests: Admin_gated_list_is_NOT_tagged_...) fails if either ever gains a tag.
`void` is NOT automatically a red flag. Seven generated functions are legitimately void -- the routes
declare 204 via the NON-GENERIC .Produces(StatusCodes.Status204NoContent) overload, or (smart-fill
validate) a deliberately empty 200. The invariant is not 'no void'. It is: VOID ONLY WHERE THE ROUTE
Counts drift as screens land, so do not treat them as an invariant -- as of M8.6: 7 tags, 24 operations,
30 models. Everything else in `TimesheetApp.Api` is still unannotated and therefore still invisible.
//ignoreUnusedModels: Keeps the emitted model set to what the included operations actually reference.
